message:
feat(membrane): add SOS barrier property tests, kernel integration test, and sobol quasi-random controller

SOS barrier testing (sos_barrier.rs, +187 lines):
- Provenance monotonicity: sweep risk (monotone decreasing) and validation
  depth (monotone increasing) across grid of (risk, depth, bloom, arena) to
  verify SOS certificate mathematical invariants
- No-panic extremes: exhaustive Cartesian product of edge values (0, 1,
  500k, 999k, 1M, MAX/2) for both provenance and quarantine evaluators
- Quarantine adverse-rate monotone degradation sweep
- Controller violation accumulation monotonicity (violations never decrease)
- Hot-path integer-only evidence: 256 rounds with varied pseudo-random inputs
  confirming no f64 creep into the evaluation path
- Full kernel integration: feed 256 cycles through RuntimeMathKernel and
  verify sos_barrier_provenance_value appears non-negative in snapshot under
  benign load
- Deterministic regression: golden value test (evaluate_provenance_barrier
  with fixed inputs must produce exactly 49990)

New controller:
- sobol.rs (597 lines): Sobol quasi-random sequence generator for low-
  discrepancy sampling in runtime_math cadence scheduling, registered in mod.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
